pr.title: Mark scratches with 100% fuzzy score as OK
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Bulk maintenance of config/GALE01/scratches.txt: scratch entries that the PR title describes as having a 100% fuzzy score were normalized from percentage-style statuses to OK, while preserving addresses, author/id/parent metadata, and timestamps. The PR had no body or review discussion, changed only the scratch-tracking config, and was merged quickly by the author.
what_changed: Changed a large number of scratch status fields in config/GALE01/scratches.txt from percent values to OK while leaving the target symbol/address and trailing metadata intact.

Examples from the diff include __init_hardware changing from 100%:0x8000541C to OK:0x8000541C, func_801E302C from 100%:0x801E302C to OK:0x801E302C, and many grIzumi/lbl/function entries around 0x801CBB88-0x801CD280 changing to OK.

Some excerpted entries with displayed percentages below 100 were also changed to OK, such as func_80003100, __fill_mem, func_801CA0B4, and func_801E3418. The slice does not explain the exact fuzzy-score computation behind those conversions.

The file also received several newly listed scratch entries, including ftCo_8009EC70 at 99.71%, ftCo_800C4550 as OK, gmMainLib_8015FBA4 at 100%, gm_80162EC8 at 100%, gm_80164840 at 74.65%, gm_8016B41C at 43.55%, gm_80174180 at 100%, and grLib_801C9EE8 at 70.43%.
Changed-files metadata reports one file, 1955 additions, 1937 deletions, and 32 hunks; the large churn is configuration/list bookkeeping rather than source-code decompilation.
smart_moves: Kept the change isolated to the scratch database, avoiding any C/ASM/source changes for a bookkeeping-only update.
Preserved scratch provenance fields such as author, id, parent, updated, and created timestamps, which keeps historical scratch lineage searchable after marking entries OK.
Used the existing scratches.txt status convention, where accepted entries are represented as OK:<address> rather than a percentage.
Retained incomplete/new scratch percentages alongside OK entries, so the file continues to distinguish accepted scratches from active candidates.
decomp_lessons: For scratch-tracking cleanup, prefer normalizing accepted matches to OK in config/GALE01/scratches.txt instead of leaving them as percentage scores; this makes future triage easier.
Bulk scratch metadata updates should preserve symbol names, addresses, ids, parent links, authors, and timestamps to avoid losing attribution and derivation history.
Do not infer source-code progress from this PR alone: it updates the scratch index, not matched C files or headers.

The title is the main evidence for the 100% fuzzy-score rule; the diff excerpt shows some displayed non-100 percentages converted to OK, so future agents should verify the scratch tool's fuzzy-score semantics before assuming the visible percentage is the only criterion.
naming_conventions: Scratch entries use the form name = status:address; // metadata.
Accepted scratch entries are represented as OK:0xADDRESS.
Unaccepted scratch entries use percentage statuses such as 99.71%:0x8009EC70 or 43.55%:0x8016B41C.

The scratch list preserves multiple naming styles already present in the project: raw func_XXXXXXXX names, lbl_XXXXXXXX labels, subsystem-prefixed names such as ftCo_, gm_, grIzumi_, and status-prefixed names such as done__ and wip__.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is about scratch-match bookkeeping rather than a new matching tactic in C or assembly.
The reusable tactic is administrative: when external scratch/fuzzy-matching tooling proves an entry acceptable, mark it OK in scratches.txt and keep all provenance comments unchanged.
Because the diff contains both OK conversions and newly added non-OK entries, future automation should separate accepted-score normalization from importing fresh scratch submissions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
No reviewer rationale beyond the PR title was available.
follow_up_queries: How does the scratch tooling compute fuzzy score versus the visible percentage stored in scratches.txt?
Are there remaining 100% entries in config/GALE01/scratches.txt that should be converted to OK?
Which script or process generated the 1955-addition/1937-deletion scratches.txt normalization?
Do newly added 100% entries such as gmMainLib_8015FBA4, gm_80162EC8, and gm_80174180 need a later OK-normalization pass?
